coordinate_system: component-side board millimetres; source-image pixels are unscaled originals
status: D56 two-sided package and three-callout geometry registered but electrically held; D15 cut pair and the D14 local ground link photo-closed; D11 endpoints and remaining D14 paths pending

drawing.observation: The position-150 leader marks the sleeved upper conductor, while three position-159 leaders reach solder locations on one row beside D56. Assembly note 11 explicitly identifies position 150 as tubing fitted at solder locations, so neither number is interpreted as a cut instruction or a net change.

package_identity.orientation: component-view notch down; solder view reflected

component_observations.observation: independent upper-overlap view shows the same marked body, notch, adjacent resistor, board edge, and pin fanout
primary close view resolves the К155АГ3 8901 marking and notch-down package
independent lower-overlap view repeats the marking, notch, adjacent resistor, board edge, and pin fanout

solder_observations.observation: the package-local reflected fit fixes D56.5 and D56.12 at the three-leader level; the separate left annulus is collinear, while visible gaps keep both package pads distinct from the nearby horizontal rail
overlap view independently repeats the left annulus, both package joints, their collinear order, and the gaps to the adjacent rail

callout_field.result: The factory detail's three position-159 locations register as the separate left annulus plus D56.5 and D56.12 at one physical level. The photographed bare-board copper leaves visible gaps between both package pads and the nearby horizontal rail, but the position-150 tubing and unexpanded position-159 callout do not prove what conductor or material was installed at assembly.

callout_identity_evidence.observation: Assembly note 11 says to fit tubing positions 157 and 150 at solder locations; this disproves the former position-150-equals-cut interpretation. The specification identity of position 159 was not photographed, so it remains a guarded solder-location callout rather than an inferred material or conductor.

d56.remaining_boundary: The three physical position-159 locations are fixed as the separate left annulus, D56.5, and D56.12. Determine the installed conductor/material and continuity among those locations and the adjacent rail directly or recover the complete item-159 specification. Position 150 remains tubing, and no visible gap is promoted as an assembly cut or source-net change.

drawing.observation: The four-hole auxiliary trace beside D15 is cut between its final two holes, aligned between visible package-pad levels 8 and 9. No replacement wire is drawn in this D15 detail.

solder_confirmation.observation: short diagonal solder-side copper reaches D15.8
short diagonal solder-side copper reaches D15.9

d15.cut_observation: Two independent component photographs show the copper removed between the upper and lower auxiliary annuli. The cut separates the original A2/A1 bridge; the clean source PCB already assigns D15.8=A2 and D15.9=A1 as distinct nets.
d15.fabrication_boundary: The locally registered landing centres are evidence for topology, not drill placement. Reproducing the original auxiliary holes requires a direct board measurement or a fabrication-grade local scale.

drawing.observation: With the notch-up package orientation fixed by both component photographs, the drawing's four-hole right row is D14.8 through D14.5 and the first four holes of its five-hole left row are D14.1 through D14.4. The fifth left-row hole is an auxiliary landing below D14.4. The position-159 leader reaches the short D14.1-side stub joining the inter-package trace.

component_observations.observation: one uninterrupted vertical component-side copper strip joins the adjacent D32.4 and D14.1 annuli
independent close view shows the same uninterrupted inter-package copper strip

ground_link.result: The observed local copper ties D14.1 to the same GND net as D32.4. The position-159 leader marks its solder location; the clean source PCB must assign D14.1 to GND.

component_observations.observation: open annulus below D14.4, separated from the four package leads
independent view repeats the same open annulus and adjacent trace geometry

auxiliary_fifth_landing.result: The fifth left-field landing is physically registered in two component views but has no accepted pin/net or fabrication drill geometry.

solder_exclusion.observation: Reflected board registration places D14 and the fifth landing inside the same heavily scraped/reworked two-row solder field in both overlapping views. The immediate right-row dogleg is hidden by the package on the component face and is not uniquely separable from the scraped solder copper on the reverse face; the available photographs are exhausted for D14.7 continuity.

d14.remaining_boundary: The fifth auxiliary landing is registered, but its conductor and the remote endpoints of the three long drawn traces and right-row dogleg remain unproved. Position 159 marks solder locations and does not prove these are replacement conductors. D14.2 and D14.7 therefore require direct continuity rather than another global photo projection, and no auxiliary drill geometry is promoted.

drawing.observation: The unique L-shaped trace and the adjacent drilled column register directly to the component-side owner photographs. The long column is an auxiliary drilled/copper field beside D11, not a drawn 14-pad package column. Position 159 points to the right-hand junction of a four-landing subfield: an upper landing reaches it by a long vertical trace, a left landing approaches through the obscured horizontal bridge, and a lower landing leaves on a separate trace.

solder_exclusion.observation: The held-out-validated reflected package fit localizes a conspicuous solder scar beside D11 pins 4 through 6, but cross-registration places the factory drawing's component-side four-landing field elsewhere. The scar is therefore not evidence for the position-159 bridge and must not be used to assign its pins or nets.

solder_photo_exhaustion.observation: A held-out-validated D11 component fit and the existing reflected solder fit replace the coarse whole-board projection. In 200506061 the package-local transform places the upper landing under the wide tinned rail and the three lower landmarks among repeated joints and parallel traces without a unique four-hole match. The same lower-field ambiguity repeats in all three overlapping solder views; 200509593 also repeats the rail obstruction, while the upper point lies outside 200522685 and 200525009. The available solder photographs are exhausted for a through-hole identity.

d11.remaining_boundary: The four component-side landing identities and the position-159 junction are registered in two views. A validated two-sided D11 package-local transform exhausts four solder views without identifying a unique corresponding four-hole field, so no through-hole or fabrication drill is accepted. The obscured bridge still does not prove a D11 pin/net or either remote trace endpoint. Preserve the source nets and require direct continuity before promoting replacement copper.
